title: hydnocarpus sumatranus var. sumatranus
intro: Hydnocarpus sumatranus var. sumatranus, also known as the Sumatran stinking nut, is a plant species native to Indonesia, belonging to the family Flacourtiaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced wound healing. Herbal preparations of Hydnocarpus sumatranus var. sumatranus often involve its seeds, roots, or leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
